---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `dashboard_definition_page.dart:1` (647 lines) contains two top-level classes (`DashboardDefinitionPage` and `EditDashboardPage`), a deeply nested `StreamBuilder`-within-`StreamBuilder` build method (lines 157–611), plus multiple `onConfirmAdd*` mutation callbacks. Split seams: extract the `FormBuilder` block into `_DashboardDefinitionForm`, extract the `ReorderableListView` section into `_DashboardItemList`, and move `EditDashboardPage` to its own thin file (`edit_dashboard_page.dart`). The corresponding test at 1 316 lines would also split naturally.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `confirmation_progress_modal_test.dart:1` (596 lines) — 11 identical boilerplate blocks of `MaterialApp`+localization delegates+`Scaffold`+`Builder`+`ElevatedButton` copied into each test. Extract a `_pumpModal({required String message, ...})` helper that pumps the shared scaffold and only exposes the varying parameters.
- [ ] **[MED]** `flags_page_test.dart:1` (1 233 lines) — 49 `pumpAndSettle` calls across tests that differ only in which flag they inspect. The 10+ "renders … flag" groups follow the same three-step pattern (pump, scroll/find, assert). Consider a `_expectFlagRow(tester, {required flag, required icon, ...})` helper invoked from a loop.
- [ ] **[MED]** `about_page_test.dart:1` — file lives at `test/features/settings/ui/pages/about_page_test.dart` but the impl is at `lib/features/settings/ui/pages/advanced/about_page.dart`. Mirror path would be `test/features/settings/ui/pages/advanced/about_page_test.dart`. **One test file per source file convention is violated.**
- [ ] **[LOW]** `dashboard_definition_page_test.dart:520–523` — `FakeDashboardDefinition` is redefined inline even though `test/mocks/mocks.dart:523` already declares it. Remove the local definition and import from the central file.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `confirmation_progress_modal_test.dart:1` — every `testWidgets` block starts with a full `MaterialApp`+`AppLocalizations.localizationsDelegates`+`AppLocalizations.supportedLocales`+`MediaQueryData`+`Scaffold`+`Builder` copy (confirmed: 11 occurrences of `localizationsDelegates`). This violates the "mock setup must not dwarf test logic" rule. Extract `_pumpModal(tester, {...params})` that hides the wrapper and exposes only the modal arguments; each test body should be ≤20 lines. None of these tests uses `makeTestableWidget()` from `widget_test_utils.dart` — they should.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `flags_page_test.dart:20` — `class FakeConfigFlag extends Fake implements ConfigFlag {}` defined locally (line 20 of the file, `class n extends Fake`). This should live in `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart` and be imported centrally. There is currently only one caller but the pattern establishes bad precedent.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `flags_page_test.dart` — setUp registers `UserActivityService` as a real instance (`UserActivityService()`), violating the "use `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()`" rule. The test uses `GetIt.I` registration boilerplate directly instead of the shared helper.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chart_multi_select_test.dart:44` — "button is tappable" test locates an `InkWell` ancestor and asserts `findsOneWidget`. This is a structural smoke check with no behavioral assertion; it does not verify that `onConfirm` is called or that the modal opens. Replace with a test that invokes `onConfirm` directly and checks the callback was invoked.
- [ ] **[MED]** `dashboard_definition_page_test.dart:271` — "then updating aggregation type in one measurement" ends with `expect(find.text(measurableChocolate.displayName), findsWidgets)` after tapping a `measurableFinder`. That assertion only confirms the widget is still visible, not that any state changed. The test should assert that the aggregation-type label changed or that a dirty-save pathway was triggered.
- [ ] **[MED]** `dashboard_category_test.dart` (top-level, 81 lines) — the only test asserts `icon.size == CategoryIconConstants.iconSizeMedium`. This is a single-property smoke check on a widget constructor argument. If this widget is worth testing, add behavioral assertions (e.g. that tapping the clear button invokes `setCategory(null)`). The file also duplicates the subject of `dashboards/dashboard_category_test.dart` — **eliminate the orphan** or merge it.
- [ ] **[LOW]** Several test groups in `flags_page_test.dart` scroll to ensure a flag row is visible, then call `pumpAndSettle` after `ensureVisible`. Because `ensureVisible` drives a scroll programmatically (no animation), a `pump()` is sufficient; the extra `pumpAndSettle` adds unnecessary settle-wait time.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `filterDisplayedFlags` (`lib/features/settings/ui/pages/flags_page.dart:58`) is a pure function whose contract is: empty/whitespace query returns the full list; non-empty query returns a stable sub-list ordered identically to the input; matching is case-insensitive and substring-based. The existing unit tests in `flags_filter_test.dart` cover hand-rolled cases but miss the **idempotence** and **monotonicity** invariants. A Glados property: "for any non-empty flag list and any query, the result is a sub-sequence of the input preserving relative order" and "applying the filter twice with the same query is idempotent" would catch regressions that hand-written examples miss.

  ```dart
  Glados2(any.flagList, any.printable, ExploreConfig(numRuns: 120)).test(
    'filtered result is always a subsequence of the input',
    (flags, query) {
      final result = filterDisplayedFlags(
        query: query, flags: flags, resolver: _stubResolver,
      );
      expect(isSubsequence(result, flags), isTrue, reason: '$query');
    },
    tags: 'glados',
  );
  ```

  (`settings_tree_index` already has Glados tests — do **not** re-add them here.)

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `sliver_box_adapter_page.dart` (109 lines) — **no test file**. `SliverBoxAdapterPage` is used as the chrome wrapper by at least `FlagsPage`, `HealthImportPage`, `LoggingSettingsPage`, etc. The `fillRemaining` flag, `showBackButton`, and scroll-behavior branches are all untested.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `outbox_badge.dart` (63 lines) — **no test file**. `OutboxBadgeIcon` contains a `Consumer` that reads `outboxStateControllerProvider` and `matrixLoginControllerProvider` and applies a grayscale color filter; the color-filter / normal-icon branch is untested.
- [ ] **[MED]** `form_text_field.dart` (43 lines) — **no test file**. The `fieldRequired` validation branch (empty-field error path) is untested.
- [ ] **[MED]** `form_switch.dart` (36 lines) — **no test file**.
- [ ] **[MED]** `entity_detail_card.dart` / `settings_card.dart` — **no test files**. Both are tiny (27 / 49 lines) decorator widgets — a single snapshot / rendering test per file would provide coverage.
- [ ] **[MED]** `habit_create_page.dart` / `measurable_create_page.dart` — **no test files**. Both are thin delegates, but the `beamToNamed` call on creation and empty-form validation branches are untested.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `flags_page_test.dart` mocks only 11 `ConfigFlag` values in `setUp`, while `FlagsBody.displayedItems` (lines 91–114) lists 22 flags. The 11 flags not present in the mock (`recordLocationFlag`, `enableTooltipFlag`, `enableLoggingFlag`, etc.) go through the `default:` arms of `_iconForFlag` / `_titleForFlag` / `_subtitleForFlag`, which are unreachable from the existing tests.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `dashboard_definition_page.dart` — the `copyDashboard` function's `DashboardSurveyItem`, `DashboardHealthItem`, and `DashboardWorkoutItem` branches in the switch all hit `break` without adding to `entityDefinitions`. These `break` branches are exercised only partially; no test verifies that a survey-item dashboard copy does not try to look up a measurable.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `confirmation_progress_modal_test.dart` — each of the 11 `testWidgets` builds and pumps a fresh `MaterialApp` + localization stack independently. Extracting a `_pumpModal` helper that shares a single `MaterialApp` across tests (or uses `makeTestableWidget`) would cut the setup cost per test by ~80 % and reduce the 19 `pumpAndSettle` calls to `pump()` in cases where no animation is involved (e.g. cancel-button dismissal, barrier tap). **Estimated impact: ~30–40 % speed-up for this file.**
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `flags_page_test.dart` — 49 `pumpAndSettle` calls, many of which follow `ensureVisible` (which does not animate) or a `tester.pump()` that already drained async work. Replacing post-`ensureVisible` settle calls with `pump()` and reducing groups that repeat the full pump-widget-from-scratch pattern to a shared `setUpAll`/`setUp` would cut runtime. **Estimated impact: ~25–35 % speed-up for this file.**
- [ ] **[MED]** `dashboard_definition_page_test.dart` — 60 `pumpAndSettle` calls in 1 316 lines. Several tests share identical 6-line mock-stub blocks (`when(mockJournalDb.getMeasurableDataTypeById(...)).thenAnswer(...)` × 3). Extracting `_stubMeasurableDb(mockJournalDb)` would also tighten individual test bodies, making it easier to spot whether settle calls can be replaced with `pump()`.
- [ ] **[MED]** `definitions_list_page_test.dart` — 28 `pumpAndSettle` calls in 372 lines. Check whether each settle is genuinely needed or whether a `pump()` suffices after an interaction that does not trigger animations.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `maintenance_page_test.dart` — 32 `pumpAndSettle` calls in 529 lines. Mostly acceptable given the `ConfirmationProgressModal` round-trips, but calls that follow `find.byType(...)` existence checks could be replaced with `pump()`.

---

## Summary

- **7 impl files have no tests** (`sliver_box_adapter_page.dart`, `outbox_badge.dart`, `form_text_field.dart`, `form_switch.dart`, `entity_detail_card.dart`, `habit_create_page.dart`, `measurable_create_page.dart`).
- **1 oversized impl** file: `dashboard_definition_page.dart` (647 lines) with clear split seams (form, item list, edit wrapper).
- **2 oversized test** files: `dashboard_definition_page_test.dart` (1 316 lines) and `flags_page_test.dart` (1 233 lines).
- **3 major weak-test issues**: `confirmation_progress_modal_test.dart` copy-pastes 11 identical boilerplate wrappers (should use `makeTestableWidget`); the orphan `ui/widgets/dashboard_category_test.dart` has one smoke assertion; `chart_multi_select_test.dart` "tappable" test asserts only structure not behavior.
- **1 Glados candidate**: `filterDisplayedFlags` — subsequence-ordering and idempotence properties.
- **3 meaningful speed wins**: reduce 49+60+11 `pumpAndSettle` calls in the three heaviest files; extracting shared helpers in `confirmation_progress_modal_test` would also cut per-test rebuild cost.
- **Biggest opportunity**: extract `_pumpModal` helper in `confirmation_progress_modal_test.dart` — eliminates 110 lines of repeated boilerplate and converts the file from an infrastructure dump to readable behavior specs.
